prompt: Cache compression trades memory for latency because smaller key value state lowers footprint, but reconstruction adds compute, bandwidth pressure, and extra decode work. Explain how hot tier retention, warm tier compression, blockwise reconstruction, prompt scaling, page pressure, prefetch behavior, quantization drift, thermal headroom, and decode throughput interact on Apple Silicon. Then restate the same idea with different phrasing: memory savings help larger prompts fit, but compressed tiers cost time when attention has to rebuild older context. Then restate it again with emphasis on system tradeoffs: exact recent tokens are cheap to read, compressed middle context saves memory, and reconstruction cost appears when long prompts or wide windows reach back into warm storage. Finally, summarize why a longer context window changes the balance between capacity, latency, and reconstruction work in a tiered KV cache.
